accept: All auto-commit messages from act write-ops follow one canonical format. Suggested: 'act-XXXX: <op_type> [<short payload>]' or 'act-op: (act-XXXX) <op_type> [<short payload>]' — pick one and use it everywhere.
The 'act- create' (no id) and 'act-act-' (double prefix) bugs are eliminated; verified via test that exercises create, claim, update, close, dep-add, redact, reopen, delete.
Doctor orphan-close detection is updated/verified to match whichever format is canonicalized.
payload.description:
Discovered 2026-05-10 by UX-eval agent. Three different commit-message shapes observed in a single sandbox session:

  act-op: act- create               (no id, missing title — looks like template/format-string bug)
  act-act-3cdc: claim 4397b575      (double 'act-' prefix)
  act-op: (act-0d9d) close          (third shape, parens, distinct from above)

The empty 'act-' in the first message and the 'act-act-' in the second are clearly bugs in the format string. Agents reading git log to understand history (or grep for issue ids) get inconsistent results. Doctor's orphan-close grep keys on '(act-XXXX)' which only matches one of the three shapes.
payload.title: auto-commit messages are inconsistent (3 formats for same op family)
